deepseek-v4-pro.error_message: The `reasoning_content` in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.

deepseek-v4-flash.error_message: The `reasoning_content` in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.

deepseek-reasoner.note: Tested with tool round-trip without reasoning_content, and it succeeded.

deepseek-chat.note: No reasoning_content at all — works without any special handling.

deepseek-v4-pro.middle_chunks: { delta: { content: null, reasoning_content: 'The user wants...' } }
deepseek-v4-pro.tool_chunks: { delta: { tool_calls: [{ index:0, id:'call_...', function:{ name:'list_dir', arguments:'' } }] } }
deepseek-v4-pro.pattern: reasoning_content streams first (full thought), THEN tool_calls delta arrives. reasoning_content is NOT in the same chunk as tool_calls.

deepseek-v4-flash.note: Same pattern as v4-pro (reasoning_content before tool_calls)

deepseek-reasoner.note: Same reasoning_content streaming pattern, but API does not require it on round-trip

deepseek-chat.note: No reasoning_content in stream at all

why_it_fails.step_1: User sends message → our extension sends POST with tools[]
why_it_fails.step_2: DeepSeek v4-pro/v4-flash streams reasoning_content chunks, then tool_calls chunks
why_it_fails.step_3: Our activeToolCalls Map captures tool_calls across chunks, emits LanguageModelToolCallPart
why_it_fails.step_4: VSCode executes the tool and calls provideLanguageModelChatResponse() again
why_it_fails.step_5: Our mapMessage() converts the assistant message from VSCode
why_it_fails.step_6a: VSCode's LanguageModelChatMessage for the assistant has ToolCallPart, but NO reasoning_content (VSCode doesn't expose it)
why_it_fails.step_6b: Our mapMessage() returns { role:'assistant', content:null, tool_calls:[...] } — NO reasoning_content field
why_it_fails.step_7: DeepSeek rejects the request: 'The reasoning_content in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.'
why_it_fails.root_cause: VSCode's LanguageModelChatMessage system has no mechanism to carry reasoning_content between calls. It only has TextPart, ToolCallPart, and ToolResultPart. We cannot attach reasoning_content to the assistant message because VSCode's API doesn't allow custom metadata on messages.

openclaude_openaiShim_ts.note: OpenClaude's openaiShim.ts does NOT handle reasoning_content at all. It only processes delta.content and delta.tool_calls. reasoning_content chunks are silently ignored.

microsoft_vscode_copilot.how: Copilot uses its own chatMLFetcher.ts (not LanguageModelChatProvider API). It captures thinking/reasoning from stream deltas and stores it for OTel telemetry, but does NOT send it back on subsequent requests.
microsoft_vscode_copilot.key_difference: Copilot has its own streaming code (not using LanguageModelChatProvider API), so it can control the entire message lifecycle.
microsoft_vscode_copilot.note: VSCode's built-in Copilot does NOT use the LanguageModelChatProvider API at all. It has its own direct networking pipeline.

openrouter.note: OpenRouter likely handles reasoning_content server-side or strips it for models that don't need it. Their AI SDK provider is a thin wrapper around the Vercel AI SDK and delegates model-specific handling upstream.

possible_fixes.approach: Capturer reasoning_content + store in module-level Map
possible_fixes.description: During streaming, capture reasoning_content chunks. Store them in a Map<toolCallId, reasoningText> at module scope (outside the class). When mapMessage encounters an assistant message with tool_calls, look up the reasoning_content from the Map and inject it.
pros: Keeps reasoning_content within our extension's memory
cons: Module-level state persists across sessions — need cleanup logic
Map key management: tool call IDs from one call won't match the next VSCode invocation
VSCode generates new call IDs on each invocation (not DeepSeek's original call IDs)
Hard to correlate which reasoning_content belongs to which tool call
possible_fixes.reason: VSCode generates its own call IDs for LanguageModelToolCallPart. The call ID in the second invocation (from ToolResultPart) won't match the original DeepSeek call ID. We'd need a different keying strategy.

possible_fixes.approach: Capture reasoning_content + append to progress.report text output
possible_fixes.description: Instead of emitting reasoning_content separately, wrap it in <thinking> tags and emit it as LanguageModelTextPart during streaming.
pros: The text becomes part of the visible conversation history
VSCode will pass it back as a TextPart on subsequent calls
cons: User sees the reasoning text in chat (may not be desirable)
Doesn't solve the API requirement — DeepSeek needs reasoning_content as a field, not as text in the content

possible_fixes.approach: Strip reasoning by using a different model parameter
possible_fixes.description: Check if DeepSeek has an API parameter to disable reasoning_content output.
pros: No code changes needed if parameter exists
cons: DeepSeek may not support disabling reasoning_content

possible_fixes.approach: Use deepseek-chat model ID when user selects v4-pro/v4-flash
possible_fixes.description: deepseek-chat routes to deepseek-v4-flash internally but WITHOUT reasoning_content. Map v4-pro and v4-flash requests to deepseek-chat internally.
pros: Simple one-line change in capabilities.ts or request builder
deepseek-chat is the same underlying model (v4-flash)
cons: User selects a specific model but gets a different one
v4-pro might have different capabilities than deepseek-chat (different routing)
Band-aid — breaks when deepseek-chat is deprecated

possible_fixes.approach: [IMPLEMENTED] Emit reasoning_content as LanguageModelDataPart on streaming + recover on round-trip
possible_fixes.description: During streaming, capture reasoning_content from delta chunks into a buffer. When tool calls are emitted, also emit a LanguageModelDataPart.json containing { reasoning_content: '...' } with mime type 'application/x-reasoning-json'. VSCode's API carries LanguageModelDataPart through the serialization cycle, so on the next invocation of provideLanguageModelChatResponse, the inbound assistant message contains a LanguageModelDataPart. mapMessage() detects it, extracts reasoning_content, and injects it into the DeepSeek API request body.

how_it_works.streaming: reasoningBuffer accumulates delta.reasoning_content across SSE chunks. After stream ends and tool calls are emitted, a final LanguageModelDataPart.json is emitted with the accumulated reasoning text.
how_it_works.round_trip: VSCode serializes the DataPart into the conversation history. On the next provideLanguageModelChatResponse call, mapMessage() iterates msg.content, finds the DataPart via instanceof vscode.LanguageModelDataPart, decodes the JSON, and adds reasoning_content to the API payload.
how_it_works.key_insight: LanguageModelDataPart is the only VSCode API primitive that survives message serialization AND allows arbitrary structured data. It's not a hack — it's the designed extensibility point for carrying metadata.

pros: Future-proof — works with any model that returns reasoning_content (v4-pro, v4-flash, future v5 models)
No model remapping needed — user selects exactly the model they want
No module-level state or Map cleanup needed — data lives in the message history
VSCode-native API (LanguageModelDataPart) — not a workaround, it's the intended extensibility path
Survives VSCode session restores and conversation history persistence
cons: Adds ~8 lines of streaming capture + ~20 lines of mapMessage recovery logic
The DataPart is technically visible in the message content (but not rendered in chat UI)
files_changed: src/extension.ts — capture reasoningBuffer during streaming, emit as DataPart, recover in mapMessage()

recommendation.long_term_fix: [IMPLEMENTED] Capture reasoning_content during streaming and emit it as a LanguageModelDataPart.json with mime 'application/x-reasoning-json'. mapMessage() recovers it on round-trip. This works because LanguageModelDataPart is the VSCode API's extensibility mechanism for carrying arbitrary structured data through message serialization.
recommendation.note: This is the permanent, future-proof fix. It works with ALL DeepSeek models (current and future) that return reasoning_content, without needing to know the model names in advance. It even works with deepseek-reasoner (which doesn't require it on round-trip but can carry it if desired). The fix does not rely on model ID mapping, so it won't break when DeepSeek deprecates model names.
files_changed: src/extension.ts — reasoningBuffer streaming capture, DataPart emission after tool calls, DataPart recovery in mapMessage()
